operating_state: Revenue reached $44.1 billion, up 69% year-over-year and 12% sequentially, driven by Data Center acceleration from large language models and generative AI applications with Blackwell architecture ramping across all customer categories. Gaming revenue increased 42% year-over-year from Blackwell architecture adoption while Professional Visualization grew 19% from Ada RTX workstation GPU sales. Supply-demand mismatches persist with manufacturing lead times exceeding 12 months amid accelerated annual product transition cadences.
cost_structure: Gross margin declined to 60.5% from 78.4% prior year due to a $4.5 billion charge for H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations following export licensing requirements, plus initial ramp costs for sophisticated Data Center systems and inventory provisions for low-yielding Blackwell material. Operating expenses increased 44% year-over-year driven by higher compensation from employee growth, increased compute infrastructure costs, and expanded engineering development resources with supplier premiums and long-term capacity commitments.
material_events: USG imposed export licensing requirements on H20 products to China effective April 9, 2025, resulting in a $4.5 billion inventory and purchase obligation charge with anticipated additional charges up to $5.5 billion in first quarter fiscal 2026. USG rescinded the AI Diffusion Interim Final Rule in May 2025 with plans for replacement rule of uncertain scope. The company repurchased 126 million shares for $14.5 billion and paid dividends.
macro_exposures: Export control restrictions eliminated quarterly H20 sales to China and expanded across multiple product categories with potential worldwide licensing requirements creating competitive disadvantages against non-US suppliers. Geopolitical tensions involving Taiwan and China threaten supply chain continuity given critical manufacturing partnerships while Chinese government investigations regarding market discrimination and Mellanox acquisition commitments pose regulatory risks.
forward_outlook: Management plans increased US-based manufacturing capabilities and specialized domestic production equipment investment dependent on domestic ecosystem capacity ramp timing. The company continues annual Data Center architecture updates and expanded supply purchases with existing and new suppliers to support demand projections. Management expects Blackwell Ultra platform samples and production units beginning Q2 fiscal 2026 while open-source foundation model adoption creates dependency on developer engagement.
uncertainty_profile: Replacement export control rules remain undefined regarding scope, timing, and product coverage with potential for broader geographic or customer restrictions on popular data center products. Product transitions and sophisticated system configurations may create supply-demand management challenges resulting in revenue volatility, quality issues, or delays. Supply chain vulnerabilities persist given concentration in Taiwan and South Korea for critical components while ongoing regulatory investigations across multiple jurisdictions create compliance burdens.
